title: Additional program packs (Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. Future work: additional program packs (Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), sequenced after the flagship. Each pack meets the flagship bar; Medicaid's expansion/waiver complexity is gated on a strong reviewer. This is a representative backlog task; the concrete program list expands as reviewers and scope are confirmed.
objective: Produce additional program guide packs at the flagship bar, sequenced after the flagship and gated on reviewer availability (Medicaid's complexity gated on a strong reviewer).
acceptanceCriteria: Each additional program pack meets the flagship bar: primary-source-cited, reading-level compliant, route-to-human coverage, expert-signed-off, no determination/structuring content
Medicaid/CHIP expansion/waiver-complexity content is gated on a strong credentialed reviewer before it ships
Programs are sequenced after the flagship guide and expand only as reviewers and scope are confirmed (representative task; not fanned out to one JSON per program until scope is bounded)
